FOUNDATION MEDICINE, INC.
FoundationOne CDx Comprehensive Genomic Profile

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
PATIENT & SPECIMEN INFORMATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Patient Name:        Yuen, Margaret
Date of Birth:       [ON FILE]
Sex:                 Female
Diagnosis:           Non-small cell lung carcinoma, adenocarcinoma
Specimen ID:         FM-REBX-2024-XXXXXX
Submitting Physician: [TREATING ONCOLOGIST]
Submitting Institution: [INSTITUTION]

Specimen Type:       Formalin-fixed paraffin-embedded (FFPE) core needle biopsy tissue
Site of Biopsy:      Right upper lobe lung mass (CT-guided core needle re-biopsy)
Date of Procedure:   [DATE]
Date Received:       [DATE]
Date of Report:      [DATE]

Primary Diagnosis Confirmed by Submitting Pathology: Invasive adenocarcinoma, TTF-1 positive; consistent with known NSCLC, adenocarcinoma histology. Specimen is adequate for analysis.

Tumor Nuclei (%):    40%
Sequencing Coverage (mean): 742x
Test Version:        FoundationOne CDx v3

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
GENOMIC BIOMARKERS (SUMMARY)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
Tumor Mutational Burden (TMB):     6 mutations/Mb   [Low-Intermediate; threshold for TMB-High: ≥10 mut/Mb]
Microsatellite Status (MSI):       MSS (Microsatellite Stable)
Homologous Recombination Deficiency (HRD): Not detected
Genomic Loss of Heterozygosity (gLOH): 8% [below HRD threshold of 16%]

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SHORT VARIANT ALTERATIONS — CLINICALLY RELEVANT (TIER I / II)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1. EGFR p.L858R (c.2573T>G) — Exon 21 — SUBSTITUTION
   Allele Frequency: 31.4%
   Classification: PATHOGENIC — Activating mutation; FDA-approved companion diagnostic biomarker
   Functional Impact: Constitutive activation of EGFR kinase; sensitizing mutation to EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitors
   Context: Driver mutation identified at diagnosis (initial allele frequency 38.2% on FoundationOne CDx, baseline). Persistent in current specimen. Reduction in allele frequency from 38.2% to 31.4% may reflect tumor heterogeneity, admixed stroma, and/or partial clonal suppression during prior osimertinib therapy.
   Therapy Association: EGFR L858R is a sensitizing EGFR mutation associated with response to osimertinib (Tagrisso), erlotinib, gefitinib, afatinib (Gilotrif), and dacomitinib. FDA-approved biomarker for osimertinib per FLAURA data.

2. EGFR p.T790M (c.2369C>T) — Exon 20 — SUBSTITUTION
   Allele Frequency: 3.1%
   Classification: PATHOGENIC (resistance) — Below standard clinical reporting threshold (typically ≥5%) but detected above analytical background noise; reported given clinical context of osimertinib resistance
   Functional Impact: Secondary gatekeeper mutation in EGFR kinase domain; confers steric hindrance preventing binding of first- and second-generation EGFR TKIs; in the setting of prior osimertinib exposure, T790M detection may represent a minor clone or reflect pre-existing subclonal T790M that emerged under selection
   Context: T790M was not detected on initial FoundationOne CDx at diagnosis. Detected at allele frequency 0.6% on concurrent liquid biopsy (Guardant360). Current tissue-based detection at 3.1% is above background; however, the very low allele frequency suggests T790M is NOT the dominant resistance mechanism. This finding must be interpreted in the context of the MET amplification (see Copy Number Alterations) and PIK3CA/PTEN alterations described below.
   Note: EGFR C797S was NOT detected. Absence of C797S argues against tertiary osimertinib resistance mediated through triple EGFR mutations (L858R/T790M/C797S).

3. PIK3CA p.E545K (c.1633G>A) — Exon 9 — SUBSTITUTION
   Allele Frequency: 18.2%
   Classification: PATHOGENIC — Oncogenic gain-of-function mutation in the PI3K catalytic subunit alpha
   Functional Impact: Activating mutation in the helical domain of PIK3CA; promotes PI3K-AKT-mTOR pathway signaling independent of upstream receptor tyrosine kinase activity; associated with resistance to EGFR TKI therapy through EGFR-independent survival signaling
   Context: NOT detected on baseline FoundationOne CDx at diagnosis. Newly acquired alteration identified in this re-biopsy specimen. The allele frequency of 18.2% indicates a substantial subclonal population. PIK3CA E545K acquisition following EGFR TKI therapy is a recognized bypass resistance mechanism that activates downstream signaling independent of EGFR.
   Co-occurrence Note: Co-occurrence of PIK3CA mutation with PTEN loss (see below) results in convergent, biallelic PI3K-AKT-mTOR pathway hyperactivation.

4. PTEN p.R130Q (c.389G>A) — Exon 5 — SUBSTITUTION
   Allele Frequency: 14.7%
   Classification: PATHOGENIC — Loss-of-function mutation in PTEN tumor suppressor
   Functional Impact: PTEN functions as a lipid phosphatase that opposes PI3K activity; R130Q disrupts catalytic activity of the phosphatase domain, resulting in constitutive AKT activation; loss of PTEN in combination with PIK3CA gain-of-function represents convergent PI3K pathway dysregulation
   Context: NOT detected on baseline FoundationOne CDx at diagnosis. Newly acquired alteration. The concurrent emergence of both PIK3CA E545K and PTEN R130Q represents a highly convergent acquired resistance mechanism driving PI3K-AKT-mTOR pathway activation following EGFR TKI-mediated selection pressure.

5. TP53 p.R248W (c.742C>T) — Exon 7 — SUBSTITUTION
   Allele Frequency: 35.1%
   Classification: PATHOGENIC — Dominant negative loss-of-function, hotspot mutation
   Functional Impact: R248W is one of the most common TP53 hotspot mutations; abrogates sequence-specific DNA binding; also has gain-of-function oncogenic properties including interference with wild-type p53 tetramers and direct activation of oncogenic transcriptional programs
   Context: Present at diagnosis (initial allele frequency 42.1%). Persistent co-mutation; allele frequency relatively stable. TP53 co-mutation in EGFR-mutant NSCLC is associated with shorter progression-free survival on EGFR TKI and more aggressive clonal evolution.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
SHORT VARIANT ALTERATIONS — VARIANTS OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE (TIER III)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

6. SMARCA4 p.R1192H — Exon 25 — SUBSTITUTION
   Allele Frequency: 16.8%
   Classification: VARIANT OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE (VUS)
   Functional Impact: SMARCA4 encodes BRG1, the ATPase subunit of the SWI/SNF chromatin remodeling complex; R1192H is a missense variant; functional impact on SWI/SNF activity not fully established; no established therapeutic association in lung adenocarcinoma at this allele frequency
   Context: Present at diagnosis (initial allele frequency not specified in initial report but noted as VUS). Persistent variant; no evidence of evolution to pathogenic status based on available functional data.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

1. MET AMPLIFICATION — HIGH-LEVEL
   Copy Number Ratio: 5.1 (relative to mean diploid reference)
   Classification: HIGH-LEVEL AMPLIFICATION (threshold for clinical significance: copy number ratio ≥2.5 or absolute copy number ≥6; this specimen meets high-level criteria)
   Note: FISH confirmation of MET amplification sent to reference laboratory as per institutional protocol; MET/CEP7 ratio result pending at time of this report.
   Functional Impact: MET amplification activates MET receptor tyrosine kinase signaling independent of EGFR; represents one of the most clinically established bypass resistance mechanisms to EGFR TKI therapy, observed in approximately 5–26% of cases of acquired EGFR TKI resistance; high-level MET amplification (gene copy number ≥6, or MET/CEP7 ratio ≥5) correlates with MET inhibitor sensitivity in preclinical and clinical data
   Context: At diagnosis, low-level MET amplification was noted (copy number ratio 2.4, not meeting threshold for high amplification). The significant increase to copy number ratio 5.1 in this re-biopsy specimen is consistent with clonal expansion of a pre-existing MET-amplified subpopulation under osimertinib selection pressure. This is the most likely dominant resistance mechanism in this patient's tumor.
   Therapy Association: MET amplification is the molecular rationale for MET inhibitor strategies (savolitinib, capmatinib, tepotinib) in combination with EGFR TKI rechallenge or as monotherapy in the post-EGFR TKI setting.

2. CCND1 GAIN
   Copy Number Ratio: 2.2
   Classification: LOW-LEVEL GAIN (copy number ratio 2.2; not meeting threshold for high amplification)
   Context: Present at diagnosis (persistent). CCND1 encodes Cyclin D1; gain may contribute to cell cycle dysregulation and reduce sensitivity to certain targeted therapies; no direct actionable clinical implication at this level.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
REARRANGEMENTS / GENE FUSIONS
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

No gene fusions or rearrangements detected.

Negative for:
  ALK rearrangement (negative)
  ROS1 rearrangement (negative)
  RET rearrangement (negative)
  NTRK1 rearrangement (negative)
  NTRK2 rearrangement (negative)
  NTRK3 rearrangement (negative)
  MET exon 14 skipping mutation (negative)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
NOTABLE NEGATIVE FINDINGS (CLINICALLY RELEVANT)
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

  EGFR C797S:       NOT DETECTED
    (Absence argues against tertiary mutation-mediated osimertinib resistance)
  EGFR exon 19 del: NOT DETECTED
  EGFR exon 20 ins: NOT DETECTED
  KRAS:             NOT DETECTED
    (Consistent with EGFR-mutant adenocarcinoma; KRAS and EGFR mutations are mutually exclusive)
  BRAF V600E:       NOT DETECTED
  ERBB2 (HER2) amplification: NOT DETECTED
  ERBB2 exon 20 insertion: NOT DETECTED
  STK11:            NOT DETECTED
  KEAP1:            NOT DETECTED
  NF1:              NOT DETECTED
  CDH1 p.E243K:     NOT DETECTED in this specimen
    (Previously reported as VUS at diagnosis; below detection threshold in current specimen; may reflect intratumoral heterogeneity or sampling differences)

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
COMPARATIVE GENOMIC ANALYSIS: BASELINE vs. PROGRESSION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

┌─────────────────────────────┬─────────────────────┬──────────────────────────┐
│ Alteration                  │ Baseline (Dx NGS)   │ Re-biopsy (This Report)  │
├─────────────────────────────┼─────────────────────┼──────────────────────────┤
│ EGFR L858R                  │ AF 38.2% (driver)   │ AF 31.4% (persistent)    │
│ TP53 R248W                  │ AF 42.1%            │ AF 35.1% (persistent)    │
│ SMARCA4 R1192H              │ Present (VUS)       │ AF 16.8% (persistent)    │
│ CDH1 E243K                  │ Present (VUS)       │ Not detected             │
│ EGFR T790M                  │ Not detected        │ AF 3.1% (LOW; new)       │
│ PIK3CA E545K                │ Not detected        │ AF 18.2% (NEW; acquired) │
│ PTEN R130Q                  │ Not detected        │ AF 14.7% (NEW; acquired) │
│ MET amplification           │ Low-level (CNR 2.4) │ HIGH-LEVEL (CNR 5.1)     │
│ CCND1 gain                  │ Low-level gain      │ Low-level gain (persist) │
│ TMB                         │ 4 mut/Mb            │ 6 mut/Mb (low-intermed.) │
│ MSI                         │ MSS                 │ MSS                      │
└─────────────────────────────┴─────────────────────┴──────────────────────────┘

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
INTERPRETATION
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

This re-biopsy specimen from a patient with EGFR L858R-mutant non-small cell lung adenocarcinoma who progressed on osimertinib (third-generation EGFR TKI) reveals a pattern of complex, polyclonal acquired resistance. The genomic findings can be organized into three mechanistic categories:

1. BYPASS SIGNALING VIA MET AMPLIFICATION (Dominant Mechanism):
   High-level MET amplification (copy number ratio 5.1), markedly increased from low-level amplification at diagnosis (CNR 2.4), represents the most clinically significant resistance mechanism. MET amplification constitutes a bypass track that activates downstream RAS-MAPK and PI3K-AKT signaling independently of EGFR suppression. The degree of amplification (CNR 5.1) crosses the threshold associated with MET inhibitor sensitivity in published clinical data (GEOMETRY mono-1, VISION, and investigator-sponsored MET-amplified NSCLC cohorts). This is consistent with clonal expansion of a pre-existing low-level MET-amplified subclone under osimertinib selection pressure. The co-occurrence of low-level T790M (AF 3.1%) in the context of high-level MET amplification and osimertinib treatment is likely a minor clonal finding and does not identify T790M as the dominant or actionable resistance driver in this case.

2. CONVERGENT PI3K-AKT-mTOR PATHWAY ACTIVATION (Secondary/Co-existing Mechanism):
   The concurrent acquisition of PIK3CA p.E545K (AF 18.2%, gain-of-function) and PTEN p.R130Q (AF 14.7%, loss-of-function) represents convergent biallelic PI3K pathway dysregulation. Both alterations are newly acquired and not present at diagnosis. Their co-emergence at similar allele frequencies suggests they may reside within the same or overlapping subclonal populations. PI3K pathway activation is a well-described EGFR TKI resistance mechanism that supports tumor cell survival downstream of EGFR and MET. The relative allele frequencies (18.2% and 14.7%) suggest that PI3K/AKT activation is operative in a substantial portion of the tumor.

3. LOW-LEVEL EGFR T790M (Minor Clone, Uncertain Clinical Significance):
   EGFR T790M is detected at AF 3.1% in tissue (above analytical noise, but well below the threshold conventionally used to define high-level T790M-mediated resistance, typically ≥10–20% tissue AF or concordant high-level ctDNA). The concurrent liquid biopsy detected T790M at 0.6% ctDNA AF. In the context of osimertinib (which itself targets T790M), high-level T790M would be paradoxical; the low-level detection here most likely represents either: (a) a minor pre-existing T790M clone that was suppressed but not fully eradicated by osimertinib, or (b) a newly emerging subpopulation whose contribution to the resistance phenotype is marginal relative to MET amplification and PI3K pathway activation. Absence of C797S confirms that tertiary on-target resistance via triple EGFR mutation (L858R/T790M/C797S) is not operative.

Overall Interpretation: This specimen demonstrates polyclonal acquired resistance to osimertinib driven predominantly by high-level MET amplification, with co-existing convergent PI3K-AKT-mTOR pathway activation through PIK3CA gain-of-function and PTEN loss-of-function. The persistence of EGFR L858R and TP53 R248W confirms the adenocarcinomatous lineage and clonal continuity with the original tumor. TMB remains low (6 mut/Mb), arguing against an immunotherapy-sensitive phenotype based solely on TMB, though concurrent PD-L1 status from the re-biopsy specimen is not provided with this NGS submission and should be assessed separately. The complex resistance landscape has significant implications for therapeutic selection and is appropriate for multidisciplinary molecular tumor board review.

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━
TECHNICAL NOTES
━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

This test was performed using the FoundationOne CDx assay, a next-generation sequencing based in vitro diagnostic device that sequences DNA from FFPE tumor tissue for the full coding sequences of 324 cancer-relevant genes, select gene rearrangements, genomic signatures (TMB, MSI, LOH), and copy number alterations across the genome. Analytical sensitivity: ≥5% allele frequency for substitutions (≥1% for select hotspots), ≥5% AF for insertions/deletions ≤40 bp. Copy number calling: amplifications defined at copy number ratio ≥2.5 or absolute copy number ≥6 relative to diploid state.

Note on low-AF variant (EGFR T790M at 3.1%): Detected above background noise but below standard clinical threshold. The detection of this variant is provided in the context of demonstrated liquid biopsy confirmation and clinical osimertinib progression; variant is judged to be tumor-derived based on allele frequency, read depth, and orthogonal ctDNA concordance. Clinical interpretation should account for the low allele frequency and probable minor clonal representation.

Specimen Adequacy: Adequate. Estimated tumor content 40% by pathology review. Sequencing coverage meets all quality thresholds. This report is clinically valid.

This report was reviewed and electronically signed by the Foundation Medicine Medical Director of Oncology.

Foundation Medicine, Inc. | Cambridge, MA
CLIA ID: 22D2027531 | CAP Accredited
